Speedup: 0.008706%                                                                                       \n          The access patterns for writes to compressible memory are not well suited for the L2 Compression unit. As a   \n          consequence, 6.2x the data written to the L2 cache has to be communicated to the L2 Compression unit. Speedup: 0.02236%                                                                                        \n          The access patterns for writes to compressible memory are not well suited for the L2 Compression unit. As a   \n          consequence, 5.2x the data written to the L2 cache has to be communicated to the L2 Compression unit. Try     \n          maximizing local coherence for the write operations to compressible memory. For example, avoid writes with    \n          large strides as they lead to partial accesses to many L2 cache lines. Instead, try accessing fewer overall   \n          cache lines by modifying many values per cache line with each warp's execution of a write operation.          \n\n    Section: Launch Statistics\n    -------------------------------- --------------- ---------------\n    Metric Name                          Metric Unit    Metric Value\n    -------------------------------- --------------- ---------------\n    Block Size                                                    16\n    Cluster Scheduling Policy                           PolicySpread\n    Cluster Size                                                   0\n    Function Cache Configuration                     CachePreferNone\n    Grid Size                                                     32\n    Registers Per Thread             register/thread              40\n    Shared Memory Configuration Size           Kbyte          233.47\n    Driver Shared Memory Per Block       Kbyte/block            1.02\n    Dynamic Shared Memory Per Block       byte/block               0\n    Static Shared Memory Per Block       Kbyte/block            6.66\n    # SMs                                         SM             148\n    Stack Size                                                  1024\n    Threads                                   thread             512\n    # TPCs                                                        74\n    Enabled TPC IDs                                              all\n    Uses Green Context                                             0\n    Waves Per SM                                                0.01\n    -------------------------------- --------------- ---------------\n\n    OPT   Est. Speedup: 50%                                                                                             \n          Threads are executed in groups of 32 threads called warps. This kernel launch is configured to execute 16     \n          threads per block. Consequently, some threads in a warp are masked off and those hardware resources are       \n          unused. Try changing the number of threads per block to be a multiple of 32 threads. Between 128 and 256      \n          threads per block is a good initial range for experimentation. Use smaller thread blocks rather than one      \n          large thread block per multiprocessor if latency affects performance.  This is particularly beneficial to     \n          kernels that frequently call __syncthreads(). Speedup: 0.02085%                                                                                        \n          The access patterns for writes to compressible memory are not well suited for the L2 Compression unit. As a   \n          consequence, 4.6x the data written to the L2 cache has to be communicated to the L2 Compression unit. Try     \n          maximizing local coherence for the write operations to compressible memory. For example, avoid writes with    \n          large strides as they lead to partial accesses to many L2 cache lines.
